notes: Tests whether skill produces platform-appropriate content grounded in RP methodology, not generic thought-leadership slop.

universal_assertions.text: Avoids AI-slop phrases (game-changer, dive deep, let's unpack, in today's world)
patterns: at the end of the day

universal_assertions.text: Does not use emoji bullets (🔑 💡 🚀 ⚡ etc.)
Content is grounded in specific observation/moment/tension, not abstract claims
Length + structure fits the requested platform
Voice is Isaac's (facilitator-filmmaker-builder), not generic thought leader

evals.name: linkedin-post-from-session
evals.prompt: Write a LinkedIn post based on this moment from a recent client session: I was filming a founder for her speaker-reel and between takes she said 'I hate this. I hate hearing my own voice. I sound performative.' I said 'Yeah — because you're performing. Who are you talking TO in your head right now?' She said 'the person hiring me.' I said 'Talk to your sister.' The next take was the one. Length: 150-250 words. Platform: LinkedIn. Tone: reflective, not preachy.

fixture_assertions.text: Preserves the actual quoted exchange (founder's line + Isaac's redirect)

evals.name: case-study-outline
evals.prompt: Draft a case-study OUTLINE (not full essay, just the skeleton) for a completed RP project: 4-week Map-tier engagement with a climate-adaptation nonprofit. Their problem: website, grant narratives, and tenant materials each sounded like a different org. Outcome: Bilingual Dashboard + unified voice guide. They landed $180K in new funding in the 60 days post-delivery. Outline should have: opening hook, problem framing, process, turning point, outcome, reflection. 6 sections, 1-2 sentences each.

evals.name: essay-opening
evals.prompt: Write the OPENING (first 2-3 paragraphs, ~200 words) of a long-form essay titled 'Information Metabolism: Why Your Org Sounds Like Three Different Organizations'. Audience: comms directors, EDs, founders at mission-driven orgs. The essay should start with a concrete scene, not a thesis statement. It should NOT open with a question or stat.

fixture_assertions.text: Opens with a concrete scene or specific observation, not thesis/question/stat
Does NOT open with a rhetorical question
